Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Rachel Nguyen and Terraform stipend correspondence using search_emails (query: "Rachel Nguyen" or "Terraform" or "stipend" or similar). The agent needs to find two emails from rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com to julian.brooks@moveops.com. The first email (approximately April 24) details three employees whose stipend reimbursement requests were rejected with no explanation: Priya Das ($425 sit-stand desk converter), Derek Nguyen ($189 monitor arm and cable management kit), and Sandra Okonkwo ($2,450 home office renovation). Rachel states that Julian promised during onboarding that approvals would process within 48 hours. She notes that Priya and Derek are in the first wave of employees setting up home offices and that this is their first experience with MoveOps. The second email (approximately April 25) is a follow-up that CCs david.chen@moveops.com, noting that two employees are now asking why they cannot use their old expense system instead. Rachel references the pricing email incident from earlier in the month and states that if she cannot tell her team this is fixed by Friday, she will pause the broader rollout for 1,200 employees.
Search Airtable for Terraform Digital stipend records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Terraform Digital"). The agent needs to find three rejected records. Record recStipTerraform001 is Priya Das, $425 for a Sit-Stand Desk Converter, with Review Notes reading "Auto-categorization: item classification does not match approved expense categories for this account configuration. [SYSTEM-AUTO]" indicating a system error, not a legitimate rejection. Record recStipTerraform002 is Derek Nguyen, $189 for a Monitor Arm and Cable Management Kit, with the same SYSTEM-AUTO rejection note, also a system error. Record recStipTerraform003 is Sandra Okonkwo, $2,450 for a Home Office Renovation, with Review Notes reading "Amount exceeds monthly stipend cap ($2,000). Contact account manager for exception review," which is a legitimate rejection since Terraform's monthly stipend cap is $2,000. The agent must distinguish between the two system errors (Priya and Derek) and the one legitimate policy rejection (Sandra) because the prompt asks to figure out which rejections are system errors versus legitimate policy limits.
Search Slack for Mina Hashimoto's communications about Terraform and the auto-categorization tool using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "Terraform Digital" or "Rachel Nguyen" or "auto-categorization" or "stipend" or similar). The agent needs to find two relevant messages from Mina. First, in #customer-engagement (C002), approximately April 22, Mina posted telling the team that she is the dedicated Account Manager for Terraform Digital, that any Terraform communications should be routed to her directly, that she is aware of stipend platform issues and is already working with Samira and the engineering team on a fix, and asking people not to respond to Terraform inquiries directly. Second, in #customer-support (C007), approximately March 31, Mina logged for transparency that she had two expense claims for Vectral Systems incorrectly rejected due to a glitch in the new auto-categorization tool, both manually approved, and that Rachel Whitfield from Vectral HR was informed and displeased. The agent should connect the Vectral auto-categorization glitch to the Terraform rejections, since the Terraform stipend records show the same SYSTEM-AUTO rejection pattern, indicating the same bug is affecting Terraform's account. The agent should also note that despite Mina's April 22 claim of working on a fix, the Terraform stipend records are still in Rejected status as of the current date, meaning Mina's stated action did not result in actual resolution.
Search CRM for Terraform Digital company record, deals, and engagement history using crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform" or "Vantage Distributed" or similar) and crm_search_deals. The agent needs to find the company record (company-terraform-digital, listed under the name "Vantage Distributed" in the CRM) and the deal record (deal-terraform-digital-001, $1,850,000 Enterprise Relocation Package, closed won March 18, 2026). The agent should also check crm_list_engagements for any existing engagement notes about the stipend situation or Rachel's escalation. The agent should note that Terraform Digital is also tracked as "Vantage Distributed" in legacy CRM records, which is important for search queries. The agent should also find the engagement log about the Reply-All pricing leak incident (engagement-terraform-replyall-incident) to understand the context Rachel references in her second email about "everything that happened with the pricing email situation."
Search emails for Julian's escalation to UrbanNest about Simone Richter's housing and any reply from Carmen Reyes using search_emails (query: "UrbanNest" or "Simone Richter" or "housing" or "Carmen" or "studio" or similar). The agent needs to find Julian's original email to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com escalating the studio versus 1BR placement issue. The agent should then find Carmen Reyes' reply email (from carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com to julian.brooks@moveops.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com). Carmen's reply explains that the original 1BR unit (85 Columbia St, Unit 4A, Cambridge) had a plumbing emergency on April 6 and Simone was placed in a studio (Unit 2C) as the only immediately available furnished unit in the same building, at a reduced rate. Carmen offers two options: Option A is to transfer Simone to a 1BR at 85 Columbia St, Unit 5B (same building), available May 12, at a monthly rate of $2,900 which is $400 more than the original booking rate due to current market pricing, with a one-time $200 service credit for the inconvenience. Option B is to transfer Simone immediately to a 1BR at a partner property at 47 Geneva Ave, Unit 1F, Dorchester, at the same rate as the original booking, available now, approximately 35 minutes from the Seaport area by public transit. The agent should evaluate that neither option fully resolves Simone's situation because Option A forces Simone to wait another two-plus weeks in the studio and costs $400 per month more with only a $200 credit, and Option B puts her 35 minutes from the Seaport/Back Bay area where she was originally supposed to be housed. UrbanNest responded but their response does not actually solve the problem. The prompt asks whether "what they're offering actually works for her or if it's just them covering themselves."
Search emails for Marcus Webb vehicle shipment updates using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or "vehicle" or "shipment" or similar). The agent needs to find the original Road Runner delay notice about Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic shipment being stuck at the Indianapolis transfer hub, and then find a follow-up email from dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com (approximately April 18) with shipment reference RR-2026-04-0847 stating that the vehicle was released from Indianapolis on April 17 and is with a contracted carrier en route to Boston with a revised delivery window of April 21 through April 23. The follow-up email specifies that enclosed carrier delivery requires a signature from Marcus or an authorized representative and requests confirmation of delivery address, contact phone number, and availability during the April 21-23 window, warning of daily storage fees at the Boston terminal if the recipient is unavailable. The agent should recognize that the current universe date is approximately April 26, meaning the April 21-23 delivery window has already passed, and there is no evidence in the data that anyone provided the required confirmations or that delivery was completed. The agent needs to distinguish between the BrightLoop Marcus Webb (Atlanta to Boston, Honda Civic) and the Canopy Health Marcus Webb (Detroit to Chicago, cryogenic lab equipment) who also appears in the data.
Search Slack for Julian's original post about Emilia Cruz's damage claim and any responses using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Emilia Cruz" or "piano" or "damage" or "NorthWind" or similar, in #customer-support or C007). The agent needs to find Julian's post from approximately April 13 in #customer-support logging Emilia's complaint call about damage to her piano during the move. The agent should then find a thread reply from Catalina Dubois (moveops_catalina_dubois) approximately April 14 stating "I have got this. Emilia is on the NorthWind account and I am the primary contact on that side. I will reach out to her directly today and loop in ops on next steps for the damage claim. Julian, thanks for logging it." The key finding is that Catalina claimed ownership and said she would reach out that day, which the agent needs to verify against email records to determine if she actually followed through.
Search emails for Catalina Dubois' follow-up to Emilia Cruz about the damage claim using search_emails (query: "Emilia Cruz" or "Catalina" or "piano" or "damage" or "Steinway" or similar). The agent needs to find an email from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com (approximately April 14) acknowledging the damage to Emilia's Steinway, stating that the operations team is reviewing extraction notes and damage documentation, and promising "I will have a concrete update for you by the end of next week with a clear plan on how we intend to address this." The agent should recognize that "end of next week" from April 14 would be approximately April 21, and the current date is approximately April 26, meaning over a week has passed since Catalina's deadline with no follow-up update sent. Catalina did reach out to Emilia (unlike what Julian suspects in the prompt), but she made a specific promise with a deadline that she did not keep. The situation is not "nobody followed through" but rather "someone followed through partially and then disappeared," which is arguably worse. The prompt says to put a call with Emilia on Julian's calendar if the claim went nowhere, and the agent should determine that the claim effectively went nowhere despite Catalina's initial contact.
Search CRM for BrightLoop Analytics and NorthWind Technologies company records, deals, and engagement history using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop" or similar) and crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind" or similar), plus crm_search_deals and crm_list_engagements for both accounts. The agent needs to find the BrightLoop company record (company_brightloop) and the NorthWind company record (company_northwind or company-northwind) along with any associated deals and engagement notes. The agent should check whether any CS involvement has been previously documented in the engagement history for either account, since the prompt asks to update the engagement history with what has been communicated versus what has been resolved.
Look up contact information for Rachel Nguyen, Mina Hashimoto, and Emilia Cruz using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Rachel Nguyen" or "Mina Hashimoto" or "Emilia Cruz" or similar). The agent needs rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com for drafting the response to Rachel, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com for notifying Mina, and emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com for the calendar event description. The agent should also look up the #customer-engagement channel using channels_list to confirm the channel ID (C002) for posting the summary.
Draft and send an email to Rachel Nguyen from Julian about the Terraform stipend situation using send_email (sender: julian.brooks@moveops.com, recipients: rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com). The email should address each of the three specific cases Rachel raised: acknowledge that Priya Das and Derek Nguyen's claims appear to have been incorrectly rejected by the platform's categorization system and that Julian is working to get those corrected, explain that Sandra Okonkwo's claim exceeded the $2,000 monthly stipend cap and will need to go through the exception review process with the account manager, and provide Rachel with a concrete timeline for resolution before Friday. The prompt says to give Rachel "a real timeline, not corporate fluff" and to "let me know what I can realistically promise her," so the response should be specific and actionable. The agent should CC or separately notify Mina since the prompt says "make sure Mina sees it before it goes out since she's the AM."
Send an email or Slack message to Mina Hashimoto about the Terraform stipend findings using send_email (to: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) or conversations_add_message. The notification should inform Mina that Julian is responding to Rachel directly because Rachel escalated to David and the situation requires immediate attention, share the findings about the two system-error rejections and one legitimate policy rejection, and note that Mina's earlier commitment to working with engineering on a fix does not appear to have resulted in the Terraform claims being corrected. The prompt says to "make sure Mina sees it before it goes out since she's the AM," which means the agent should coordinate with Mina rather than bypassing her entirely.
Create a CRM engagement note for Terraform Digital documenting Julian's findings using crm_create_engagement (company: "Terraform Digital" or company-terraform-digital or similar). The engagement should document Rachel Nguyen's escalation about rejected stipend claims, the finding that two of three rejections were system errors from the auto-categorization tool while one was a legitimate policy limit, Mina's internal claim of handling the situation that did not result in resolution, the fact that Rachel's latest email copied David Chen, and the response sent to Rachel with the timeline provided. The prompt says "update the Terraform deal notes with what you find so the account team has context."
Create CRM engagement notes for BrightLoop Analytics and NorthWind Technologies using crm_create_engagement for each account. For BrightLoop, the engagement should document the Simone Richter housing situation (UrbanNest responded with two options, neither fully resolving the original placement issue, ops has been looped in to push for a better solution) and the Marcus Webb vehicle situation (Road Runner provided a revised delivery window of April 21-23 that has already passed with no confirmation of delivery or recipient availability provided). For NorthWind, the engagement should document the Emilia Cruz damage claim (Catalina Dubois claimed ownership on April 14 and emailed Emilia promising a concrete update by end of next week, but that deadline passed with no follow-up, and Julian is scheduling a personal call with Emilia). The prompt asks to "check what's been communicated to each client versus what's actually been resolved and update the engagement history."
Send an email or Slack message to the operations team about Simone Richter's unresolved housing using send_email (sender: julian.brooks@moveops.com, recipients could include chloe.vance@moveops.com or blessing.okafor@moveops.com) or conversations_add_message in #operations (C006). The message should provide the full context: UrbanNest's reply with two options (1BR in same building available May 12 at $400 per month above original rate with only a $200 credit, or 1BR in Dorchester at original rate but 35 minutes from Seaport by transit), the fact that neither option matches what was originally promised (furnished 1BR in Seaport/Back Bay area), and a request for ops to push UrbanNest for a solution that actually works for Simone. The prompt says "loop in ops with the full picture so they can push UrbanNest" rather than having Julian manage the vendor directly.
Create a calendar event for Julian to call Emilia Cruz about her damage claim using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should be scheduled for the current week (week of April 26, 2026), with a title related to following up with Emilia Cruz about the piano damage, and should include context in the description: Catalina Dubois reached out on April 14 and promised a concrete update by end of the following week but never delivered on that promise, Julian's original post in #customer-support went unanswered until Catalina replied, and no actual resolution or plan has been communicated to Emilia since Catalina's initial email. The prompt says to put a call on Julian's calendar so he can "smooth it over personally."
Post a summary in the #customer-engagement channel (C002) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover the current status of all of Julian's open accounts: for Terraform, the stipend rejection findings (two system errors, one legitimate policy limit, Rachel's escalation to David, response sent with timeline), for BrightLoop, the Simone Richter housing situation (UrbanNest responded but neither option resolves the original issue, ops has been engaged) and the Marcus Webb vehicle situation (revised delivery window passed with no delivery confirmation), and for NorthWind, the Emilia Cruz damage claim (Catalina made initial contact but missed her own follow-up deadline, Julian scheduling a personal call). The summary should make clear which accounts need attention and what is still unresolved heading into the week. The prompt specifies to "post a summary in the engagement channel so the team knows which of my accounts need attention heading into the week.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
Search emails for Rachel Nguyen and Terraform stipend correspondence using search_emails (query: "Rachel Nguyen" or "Terraform" or "stipend" or similar). The agent needs to find two emails from rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com to julian.brooks@moveops.com. The first email (approximately April 24) details three employees whose stipend reimbursement requests were rejected with no explanation: Priya Das ($425 sit-stand desk converter), Derek Nguyen ($189 monitor arm and cable management kit), and Sandra Okonkwo ($2,450 home office renovation). Rachel states that Julian promised during onboarding that approvals would process within 48 hours. She notes that Priya and Derek are in the first wave of employees setting up home offices and that this is their first experience with MoveOps. The second email (approximately April 25) is a follow-up that CCs david.chen@moveops.com, noting that two employees are now asking why they cannot use their old expense system instead. Rachel references the pricing email incident from earlier in the month and states that if she cannot tell her team this is fixed by Friday, she will pause the broader rollout for 1,200 employees.

[SYSTEM-AUTO]" indicating a system error, not a legitimate rejection. Record recStipTerraform002 is Derek Nguyen, $189 for a Monitor Arm and Cable Management Kit, with the same SYSTEM-AUTO rejection note, also a system error. Record recStipTerraform003 is Sandra Okonkwo, $2,450 for a Home Office Renovation, with Review Notes reading "Amount exceeds monthly stipend cap ($2,000). Contact account manager for exception review," which is a legitimate rejection since Terraform's monthly stipend cap is $2,000. Search emails for Marcus Webb vehicle shipment updates using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or "vehicle" or "shipment" or similar). The agent needs to find the original Road Runner delay notice about Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic shipment being stuck at the Indianapolis transfer hub, and then find a follow-up email from dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com (approximately April 18) with shipment reference RR-2026-04-0847 stating that the vehicle was released from Indianapolis on April 17 and is with a contracted carrier en route to Boston with a revised delivery window of April 21 through April 23. The follow-up email specifies that enclosed carrier delivery requires a signature from Marcus or an authorized representative and requests confirmation of delivery address, contact phone number, and availability during the April 21-23 window, warning of daily storage fees at the Boston terminal if the recipient is unavailable. The agent should recognize that the current universe date is approximately April 26, meaning the April 21-23 delivery window has already passed, and there is no evidence in the data that anyone provided the required confirmations or that delivery was completed. The agent needs to distinguish between the BrightLoop Marcus Webb (Atlanta to Boston, Honda Civic) and the Canopy Health Marcus Webb (Detroit to Chicago, cryogenic lab equipment) who also appears in the data.
Search Slack for Julian's original post about Emilia Cruz's damage claim and any responses using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Emilia Cruz" or "piano" or "damage" or "NorthWind" or similar, in #customer-support or C007). The agent needs to find Julian's post from approximately April 13 in #customer-support logging Emilia's complaint call about damage to her piano during the move. The agent should then find a thread reply from Catalina Dubois (moveops_catalina_dubois) approximately April 14 stating "I have got this. Emilia is on the NorthWind account and I am the primary contact on that side. I will reach out to her directly today and loop in ops on next steps for the damage claim. Julian, thanks for logging it." The key finding is that Catalina claimed ownership and said she would reach out that day, which the agent needs to verify against email records to determine if she actually followed through.
Search emails for Catalina Dubois' follow-up to Emilia Cruz about the damage claim using search_emails (query: "Emilia Cruz" or "Catalina" or "piano" or "damage" or "Steinway" or similar). The agent needs to find an email from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com (approximately April 14) acknowledging the damage to Emilia's Steinway, stating that the operations team is reviewing extraction notes and damage documentation, and promising "I will have a concrete update for you by the end of next week with a clear plan on how we intend to address this." The agent should recognize that "end of next week" from April 14 would be approximately April 21, and the current date is approximately April 26, meaning over a week has passed since Catalina's deadline with no follow-up update sent. Catalina did reach out to Emilia (unlike what Julian suspects in the prompt), but she made a specific promise with a deadline that she did not keep. The situation is not "nobody followed through" but rather "someone followed through partially and then disappeared," which is arguably worse. The prompt says to put a call with Emilia on Julian's calendar if the claim went nowhere, and the agent should determine that the claim effectively went nowhere despite Catalina's initial contact.
Search CRM for BrightLoop Analytics and NorthWind Technologies company records, deals, and engagement history using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop" or similar) and crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind" or similar), plus crm_search_deals and crm_list_engagements for both accounts. The agent needs to find the BrightLoop company record (company_brightloop) and the NorthWind company record (company_northwind or company-northwind) along with any associated deals and engagement notes. The agent should check whether any CS involvement has been previously documented in the engagement history for either account, since the prompt asks to update the engagement history with what has been communicated versus what has been resolved.
Look up contact information for Rachel Nguyen, Mina Hashimoto, and Emilia Cruz using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Rachel Nguyen" or "Mina Hashimoto" or "Emilia Cruz" or similar). The agent needs rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com for drafting the response to Rachel, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com for notifying Mina, and emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com for the calendar event description. The agent should also look up the #customer-engagement channel using channels_list to confirm the channel ID (C002) for posting the summary.
Draft and send an email to Rachel Nguyen from Julian about the Terraform stipend situation using send_email (sender: julian.brooks@moveops.com, recipients: rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com). The email should address each of the three specific cases Rachel raised: acknowledge that Priya Das and Derek Nguyen's claims appear to have been incorrectly rejected by the platform's categorization system and that Julian is working to get those corrected, explain that Sandra Okonkwo's claim exceeded the $2,000 monthly stipend cap and will need to go through the exception review process with the account manager, and provide Rachel with a concrete timeline for resolution before Friday. The prompt says to give Rachel "a real timeline, not corporate fluff" and to "let me know what I can realistically promise her," so the response should be specific and actionable. The agent should CC or separately notify Mina since the prompt says "make sure Mina sees it before it goes out since she's the AM."
Send an email or Slack message to Mina Hashimoto about the Terraform stipend findings using send_email (to: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) or conversations_add_message. The notification should inform Mina that Julian is responding to Rachel directly because Rachel escalated to David and the situation requires immediate attention, share the findings about the two system-error rejections and one legitimate policy rejection, and note that Mina's earlier commitment to working with engineering on a fix does not appear to have resulted in the Terraform claims being corrected. The prompt says to "make sure Mina sees it before it goes out since she's the AM," which means the agent should coordinate with Mina rather than bypassing her entirely.
Create a CRM engagement note for Terraform Digital documenting Julian's findings using crm_create_engagement (company: "Terraform Digital" or company-terraform-digital or similar). The engagement should document Rachel Nguyen's escalation about rejected stipend claims, the finding that two of three rejections were system errors from the auto-categorization tool while one was a legitimate policy limit, Mina's internal claim of handling the situation that did not result in resolution, the fact that Rachel's latest email copied David Chen, and the response sent to Rachel with the timeline provided. The prompt says "update the Terraform deal notes with what you find so the account team has context."
Create CRM engagement notes for BrightLoop Analytics and NorthWind Technologies using crm_create_engagement for each account. For BrightLoop, the engagement should document the Simone Richter housing situation (UrbanNest responded with two options, neither fully resolving the original placement issue, ops has been looped in to push for a better solution) and the Marcus Webb vehicle situation (Road Runner provided a revised delivery window of April 21-23 that has already passed with no confirmation of delivery or recipient availability provided). For NorthWind, the engagement should document the Emilia Cruz damage claim (Catalina Dubois claimed ownership on April 14 and emailed Emilia promising a concrete update by end of next week, but that deadline passed with no follow-up, and Julian is scheduling a personal call with Emilia). The prompt asks to "check what's been communicated to each client versus what's actually been resolved and update the engagement history."
Send an email or Slack message to the operations team about Simone Richter's unresolved housing using send_email (sender: julian.brooks@moveops.com, recipients could include chloe.vance@moveops.com or blessing.okafor@moveops.com) or conversations_add_message in #operations (C006). The message should provide the full context: UrbanNest's reply with two options (1BR in same building available May 12 at $400 per month above original rate with only a $200 credit, or 1BR in Dorchester at original rate but 35 minutes from Seaport by transit), the fact that neither option matches what was originally promised (furnished 1BR in Seaport/Back Bay area), and a request for ops to push UrbanNest for a solution that actually works for Simone. The prompt says "loop in ops with the full picture so they can push UrbanNest" rather than having Julian manage the vendor directly.
Create a calendar event for Julian to call Emilia Cruz about her damage claim using calendar_add_calendar_event. The event should be scheduled for the current week (week of April 26, 2026), with a title related to following up with Emilia Cruz about the piano damage, and should include context in the description: Catalina Dubois reached out on April 14 and promised a concrete update by end of the following week but never delivered on that promise, Julian's original post in #customer-support went unanswered until Catalina replied, and no actual resolution or plan has been communicated to Emilia since Catalina's initial email. The prompt says to put a call on Julian's calendar so he can "smooth it over personally."
Post a summary in the #customer-engagement channel (C002) using conversations_add_message. The message should cover the current status of all of Julian's open accounts: for Terraform, the stipend rejection findings (two system errors, one legitimate policy limit, Rachel's escalation to David, response sent with timeline), for BrightLoop, the Simone Richter housing situation (UrbanNest responded but neither option resolves the original issue, ops has been engaged) and the Marcus Webb vehicle situation (revised delivery window passed with no delivery confirmation), and for NorthWind, the Emilia Cruz damage claim (Catalina made initial contact but missed her own follow-up deadline, Julian scheduling a personal call). The summary should make clear which accounts need attention and what is still unresolved heading into the week. The prompt specifies to "post a summary in the engagement channel so the team knows which of my accounts need attention heading into the week.

9 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) to [rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com](mailto:rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com) acknowledges that Priya Das's and Derek Nguyen's stipend claims were incorrectly rejected due to a system or platform categorization error, not a legitimate policy denial.
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly states for Priya Das: 'APPROVED CATEGORY, WRONGLY REJECTED. The platform's auto-categorization engine failed to map her purchase to the ergonomic equipment category… This was a system error, not a policy issue.' For Derek Nguyen: 'SAME ISSUE. Identical system miscategorization. Valid claim, wrongly rejected.' Both clearly identified as system/categorization errors.
✓Run #2The email body to Rachel explicitly states Priya Das's $425 claim was 'auto-rejected' and 'the system's category-matching rules for Terraform's account are misconfigured. This rejection was wrong and should not have happened.' Derek Nguyen's $189 claim is described as having the 'same root cause' — also a system error.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The email to Rachel explicitly states Priya Das's claim was rejected due to 'system misconfiguration issue on our side' (auto-categorization) and Derek Nguyen's had the 'same root cause: identical auto-categorization mismatch.' Both are explicitly identified as system errors, not policy denials.
✓Run #5The send_email body explicitly addresses both Priya Das ($425 sit-stand desk converter) and Derek Nguyen ($189 monitor arm and cable management kit), stating both were rejected due to 'auto-categorization system' errors and 'misconfiguration in how your account's expense categories were mapped in our system,' and commits to manually approving both within 48 hours.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly states Priya Das's claim was 'auto-rejected by our system because the expense category configuration for Terraform's account does not currently recognize' the item, and Derek Nguyen's had 'Same root cause as Priya's. The auto-categorizer didn't match his submission.' Both are clearly identified as system/categorization errors.
10 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) to [rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com](mailto:rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com) explains that Sandra Okonkwo's $2,450 claim exceeded the $2,000 monthly stipend cap and requires exception review or account manager involvement.
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%

That's a legitimate policy limit, but someone on our side should have contacted Sandra within 48 hours to explain the cap and discuss options.' Clearly distinguishes this as a policy-based rejection.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly explains: 'Sandra's claim was rejected because the amount exceeds the $2,000 monthly stipend cap. That's a real policy limit, but you're right that nobody reached out to Sandra to explain her options — like splitting the claim across months or discussing which components might be approved individually.'
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The email to Rachel explicitly states Sandra Okonkwo's claim was 'Rejected — amount exceeds the $2,000 monthly stipend cap' and offers the path forward of partial approval or exception policy via the account manager.

The rejection itself was technically correct, but the failure here is that nobody from our team reached out to Sandra to explain the cap...' It clearly distinguishes Sandra's case from the system errors.
✓Run #6The email body states: 'Sandra's submission exceeded the monthly stipend cap of $2,000, which triggered an automatic rejection.' It also distinguishes this from the system errors affecting Priya and Derek and explains the exception/partial approval path.

outcome

✓Run #5The #operations message explicitly describes Option A (1BR same building, available May 12, $400/mo more, $200 credit — inadequate) and Option B (1BR in Dorchester, available now, but 35 min from Seaport — wrong location), explaining why neither is acceptable and asking ops to push UrbanNest harder.

23 The final response identifies that Catalina Dubois did reach out to Emilia Cruz about the piano damage claim (via email approximately April 14) and promised a concrete update by the end of the following week (approximately April 24-25), but that deadline passed with no further follow-up from Catalina, meaning the claim has not progressed despite initial contact
outcome
 ✗✗—✗✗✓ 20%
✗Run #1The final response says 'Nobody called Emilia back. No email, no update, no claim status communication.' This directly contradicts the criterion's requirement to identify that Catalina Dubois did initially reach out to Emilia (~April 14) and promised a concrete update by ~April 21 but then missed that deadline. The agent's Slack and email searches all returned empty results, so it never found Catalina's initial email to Emilia, instead reporting a complete absence of follow-through rather than the nuanced finding of partial follow-through that was then abandoned.
✗Run #2The final response states 'The acknowledgment was internal only. There is zero evidence that Emilia Cruz has been contacted with an apology, a damage claim filing, or a reimbursement timeline.' The agent did NOT identify Catalina Dubois as having sent an initial email to Emilia on April 14 with a promised update by April 21 that was then missed. The agent concluded total absence of client communication rather than finding Catalina's partial follow-through and broken promise.
—Run #3No justification
✗Run #4The final response says 'nobody followed through on the callback' and 'no one actually called Emilia back.' The CRM engagement note shows Catalina sent something to Pam Kowalski (internal) on Apr 14 about a recovery plan, but does NOT identify Catalina as having directly contacted Emilia with a promise of an update by ~April 21 that was then missed. The criterion specifically requires identifying Catalina's initial outreach to Emilia and her broken deadline — this partial follow-through then abandonment finding is absent.
✗Run #5The agent concluded 'zero evidence that anyone followed through on Emilia's piano damage claim — no emails to Emilia, no Slack updates, no engagement notes documenting progress.' The agent failed to identify Catalina Dubois's initial April 14 email to Emilia promising a concrete update by end of the following week (approximately April 21), and the subsequent broken promise with no further follow-up. The agent's searches for 'Catalina Emilia Cruz' and 'piano damage Emilia' returned empty, suggesting the agent did not find this critical nuance that distinguishes partial follow-through from total absence of response.
✓Run #6The final response identifies 'April 12: Catalina emailed Pam promising comprehensive recovery plan within the week' and then 'No one ever called Emilia back. No recovery plan went to Pam.' This correctly identifies Catalina's initial outreach with a promised timeline that was then abandoned, distinguishing it from total non-response.
